name: Ardal and Tinjarra Micro-reserve Trail
name_original: Valle del río Tús — circular desde Yeste
description: This difficult 22.1-mile mountain biking route in the Tús River Valley near Yeste offers challenging terrain and stunning views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Yeste are set within the rugged Sierra de Alcaraz and Segura, characterized by dramatic limestone rock walls and rivers carving through the terrain. The region features extensive Mediterranean forests and varied elevations, providing a diverse landscape for mountain biking. This mountainous environment offers a mix of challenging ascents, technical sections, and forest paths. Best mountain bike trails around Yeste The most popular mountain bike trail is Tús River Valley - circular from Yeste, a 22.1 miles (35.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the scenic Tús River Valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Candelabra Pine loop from Yeste, a difficult 16.6 miles (26.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through forested areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo de Yeste – View from Molejón de Paúles loop from Yeste, a 20.7 miles (33.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of historic landmarks, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Mountain biking around Yeste is defined by rugged limestone landscapes, Mediterranean forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Yeste are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 15 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Yeste's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain biking terrain can I expect around Yeste?
A: Mountain biking around Yeste offers a diverse landscape within the rugged Sierra de Alcaraz and Segura. You'll encounter dramatic limestone rock walls, rivers carving through the terrain, and extensive Mediterranean forests. The trails feature a mix of challenging ascents, technical sections, and forest paths, with varied elevations providing a rewarding experience for different skill levels.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Yeste?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Yeste documented on komoot, offering a range of experiences. These include 8 moderate and 10 difficult routes, ensuring options for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Yeste?
A: While many routes around Yeste are classified as moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes, there are no trails specifically marked as 'easy' in the komoot data. Riders with families should assess the difficulty and elevation profiles of moderate routes carefully to determine suitability for their group.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain biking in Yeste?
A: The mountain bike trails in Yeste are generally categorized as moderate to difficult. You can expect challenging ascents and technical sections, with some routes requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills, especially those described as 'hard gravel rides' or traversing rugged limestone landscapes. The region's trails often align with the Single Track Scale (STS), indicating varying technical demands.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Yeste?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Yeste are circular. For example, you can explore the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187150590/tus-river-valley-circular-from-yeste" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tús River Valley - circular from Yeste&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31461644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Candelabra Pine loop from Yeste&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain through forested areas.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can find impressive natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt;, known for its magnificent waterfalls such as the 'Cascada de los Batanes' and 'Los Chorros waterfall'. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2745212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de Las Canales&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5974287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Chorros Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The trails also offer scenic viewpoints, such as those around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3484172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, you can incorporate historical sites into your rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31461751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Yeste – View from Molejón de Paúles loop from Yeste&lt;/a&gt; leads through varied terrain with views of historic landmarks, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Yeste&lt;/a&gt; itself. The region also features other castles like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4577208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Yedra Ruins&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Yeste?
A: The mountain biking experience in Yeste is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 15 reviews. Riders often praise the rugged limestone landscapes, the extensive Mediterranean forests, and the scenic river valleys that define the area. The diverse network of trails, offering options from moderate to difficult, is also a significant draw.

Q: Is it possible to find routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. Yeste's mountainous topography means many routes feature significant elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31458755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Moheda Bridge loop from Cascada del Arroyo de los Marines&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1100 meters of ascent, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural spring – Venta de Ticiano loop from Hospederia Rio Zumeta&lt;/a&gt; has over 1200 meters of climbing, providing a challenging workout for experienced riders.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in Yeste?
A: The provided information does not indicate any specific permits required for mountain biking on the general trails around Yeste. However, it's always advisable to check with local tourism offices or park authorities for the most current regulations, especially if riding within protected natural areas like the Parque Natural de Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Yeste?
A: Yeste is located in a region with a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. These seasons provide ideal conditions to enjoy the diverse landscapes and challenging trails.

The views from the top are worth it.
Picturesque path through the valley of the river Tús. On one side we have a rock wall and on the other - a sea of pine trees.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride, requiring very good physical condition. The terrain is rugged, typical of limestone landscapes, and can include a mix of wider tracks and narrower, unpaved paths. Expect rocky sections, dirt paths, and potentially muddy areas, especially near the river.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Tús River Valley circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: On average, completing this 35.5-kilometer route takes approximately 3 hours and 10 minutes of riding time. However, given its difficult rating and significant elevation gain, plan for a longer overall duration to account for breaks and enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What notable sights or natural features can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning views of narrow valleys, high mountains, and Mediterranean forests within the Sierra de Segura. Key highlights include a Natural viewpoint, the historic La Moheda Bridge, and the picturesque Tús River Valley itself, known for its crystal-clear waters and dramatic gorges.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Tús River Valley circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting from Yeste, you can typically find parking within or near the town. Specific parking areas for trail access are usually well-marked. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival in Yeste for the most convenient and designated parking spots.

faqs.question: Is the Tús River Valley circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in natural parks in Spain, they are typically required to be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Tús River Valley circular route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potential for rain or snow at higher elevations, making the rugged terrain more challenging.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific entrance fees for the Parque Natural de los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima, nor are permits generally required for mountain biking on established trails like this one. However, always respect local regulations and signage, especially concerning environmental protection.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for mountain biking within the Parque Natural de los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima?
faqs.answer: Yes, as part of a Natural Park, visitors are expected to follow specific regulations. This includes staying on marked trails, not disturbing wildlife or plants, carrying out all trash, and respecting quiet hours. Mountain biking is generally permitted on designated routes, but off-trail riding is prohibited to protect the delicate ecosystem.

faqs.question: Does the Tús River Valley circular route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that overlap with official routes such as GR-68, GR-66 Sendero Calar de la Sima, and GR-66 Sendero Calar del Mundo, among others like Monte Ardal and Circular Fuensanta. This offers opportunities to explore further if you wish to extend your ride.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation available in Yeste?
faqs.answer: Yeste is a local town that serves as a good base for exploring the region. You can expect to find basic amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and various accommodation options, including guesthouses and rural lodgings, suitable for mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain biking trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and remote sections, it's essential to bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions. A GPS device or a fully charged phone with offline maps is also highly recommended due to the challenging terrain and potential for limited cell service.
